name: cisco7crack
title: cisco7crack
summary: Crypt and decrypt the cisco type 7 passwords
description:
Crypt and decrypt the cisco type 7 passwords
This tool is used to crack Cisco Type 7 passwords. Can be used to encrypt
and decrypt Cisco device passwords.
.
Originally designed in order to allow quick decryption of stored passwords,
Type 7 passwords are not a secure form of password storage. There are many
tools available that can easily decrypt these passwords. Use of Type 7
passwords should be avoided unless required by a feature that is in use on
the Cisco IOS device.

commands.name: cisco7crack
commands.description: Crypt and decrypt the cisco type 7 passwords

arguments.description: display all the ways to crypt <plaintext>
arguments.raw: -a  display all the ways to crypt <plaintext>

arguments.description: cause cisco7crack to be really quiet
arguments.raw: -q  cause cisco7crack to be really quiet

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cisco7crack because Crypt and decrypt the cisco type 7 passwords.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cisco7crack as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cisco7crack [OPTIONS] [ARGS] ...` as documented upstream for cisco7crack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cisco7crack -c#3 '@l1c3&b0b'` as documented upstream for cisco7crack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cisco7crack -c#3 -q n0v3rb0s3` as documented upstream for cisco7crack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cisco7crack 082F1C5A1A490D43000F5E033F78373B` as documented upstream for cisco7crack.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cisco7crack with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cisco7crack --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
